frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: Normalization weights are dynamically retuned to homeostatically maintain response products.

frameworks.name: homeostatic plasticity
frameworks.one_line: Adaptation maintains pairwise response products as a learned target.

frameworks.name: feedforward gain control
frameworks.one_line: Multi-layer feedforward gain models that the authors find less consistent with data.

frameworks.name: homeostatic covariance/correlation models
frameworks.one_line: Variants maintaining correlations or covariance instead of products.

core_question: What learning rule governs orientation adaptation in V1 to explain both gain changes and tuning shifts?
key_assumptions: Adaptation reflects homeostatic regulation of pairwise response statistics
Divisive normalization weights are plastic on adaptation timescales
V1 neurons have orientation tuning shaped by normalization pool
manipulations: Adapter orientation relative to neuron's preferred orientation
Choice of homeostatic target (product vs covariance vs correlation)
Model architecture (recurrent normalization vs multi-layer feedforward)
key_findings: Neurons near adapter are suppressed and shift preferred orientation away from adapter
A simple learning rule reweighting normalization reproduces these effects
Simulations closely match physiological adaptation data
interpretations: Homeostatic maintenance of response products best accounts for V1 adaptation phenomena
Adaptation is a dynamic adjustment of normalization weights rather than feedforward gain alone
